refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR used the phrase 'support achieving,' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies implementation. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's functional capability without specifying how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single functional objective related to energy efficiency.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through measurement that the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target over the agreed evaluation period.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy use per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use and throughput
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish a configurable energy efficiency target parameter for system tuning
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates measurement and control functions, enabling modular design. Coupling is minimized by defining clear interfaces for energy data and control actions.

assumptions_risks: Energy measurement accuracy is sufficient to reliably assess energy_per_ton
Operational policies remain stable and compatible with control strategies
Feedback control can respond effectively within the evaluation period
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for configurable energy efficiency target parameter to support customer-defined targets and system tuning.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as control feedback is localized.
Contains physical energy consumption but monitoring and feedback logic may diffuse to other modules.
Limited containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not energy management.
Safety may use energy data but does not contain the monitoring and control logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control DP, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid unintended diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control mechanisms to meet the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause some coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid unintended diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with a containment score of 9 and low diffusion score of 2, indicating that the DP is well contained within this IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and network link interfaces that provide real-time energy consumption and system status, enabling measurement of energy_per_ton against the target over the evaluation period.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, centralizing energy management functions and minimizing diffusion across other modules.

recommended_revisions: Define and document clear interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to prevent unintended diffusion and coupling.
Establish mechanisms to manage and monitor dependencies on the Vehicle module for raw energy data to reduce coupling risks.
Consider periodic review of module containment and diffusion scores as system evolves to ensure continued architectural integrity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear verification feasibility and appropriate allocation of design parameters to implementation modules. The containment and diffusion scores indicate a well-contained solution with manageable coupling risks. Recommended revisions focus on interface documentation and dependency management rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining architectural integrity through monitoring is advised, but no further sub-FR decomposition is necessary at this stage.

revise.why: To define and document clear interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link and establish mechanisms to manage dependencies on the Vehicle module, reducing coupling risks and preventing unintended diffusion.

assumptions: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' fully satisfies the top-level FR.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces can be clearly defined and controlled.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate architectural boundary for this functionality.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition.
Interface diffusion risks with Telemetry and Network Link modules.
evidence_needed: Verification data from telemetry and network link interfaces showing energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
Periodic containment and diffusion score assessments to monitor architectural integrity.
